revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「…」
revision.text:
'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「職員令」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== 概要 == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。[[類聚三代格]]によると[[808年]]（[[大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[漆部]] *[[漆]] *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]]

'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「職員令」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== 概要 == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。[[類聚三代格]]によると[[808年]]（[[大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]]

revision.text:
'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省#律令制における大蔵省|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== 概要 == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:大蔵省]]

'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省#律令制における大蔵省|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== 概要 == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。

'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== 概要 == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。

revision.comment: Created page with "== Overview == * A general term for multiple {{ill|lt=Beminsei|1=Bemin|2=ja|3=部民制}} groups or the entirety of such groups. * Before the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|1=Taika|2=ja|3=大化}}, it referred to occupational groups directly attached to the court, including {{ill|lt=Nashiro|1=Nashiro|2=ja|3=名代}}. * After the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|1=Taika|2=ja|3=大化}} and during the {{ill|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō|2=ja|3=律令制}}, it referred to those assigned to g..."
revision.text:
== Overview == * A general term for multiple {{ill|lt=Beminsei|1=Bemin|2=ja|3=部民制}} groups or the entirety of such groups. * Before the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|1=Taika|2=ja|3=大化}}, it referred to occupational groups directly attached to the court, including {{ill|lt=Nashiro|1=Nashiro|2=ja|3=名代}}. * After the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|1=Taika|2=ja|3=大化}} and during the {{ill|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō|2=ja|3=律令制}}, it referred to those assigned to government offices to produce goods and pass down technical skills for use in the imperial court. They were considered the successors of the occupational groups of the pre-Taika period.
Shinabe had multiple meanings as described above.
Before the Taika Reform, they were led by powerful families like the {{ill|lt=Tomobe|1=Tomobe|2=ja|3=伴造}} and served the Yamato court ({{ill|lt=Yamato polity|1=Yamato Ōken|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権}}) by providing various goods and labor. Many artisans among the {{ill|lt=Toraijin|1=Toraijin|2=ja|3=渡来人}}, who arrived in Japan after the latter half of the 5th century, were also classified as Shinabe. After the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|1=Taika no Kaishin|2=ja|3=大化の改新}}, some were abolished, while others were reorganized under government offices and required to produce luxury goods and specialized industrial products for the court as part of taxation. Under the {{ill|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Ritsuryō|2=ja|3=大宝律令}}, the {{ill|lt=Shikinenryō|1=Shikinenryō|2=ja|3=職員令}} defined their affiliation, names, and legal household numbers as follows:
* {{ill|lt=Toshoryō|1=Toshoryō|2=ja|3=図書寮}} - Paper households (50) * {{ill|lt=Gagakuryō|1=Gagakuryō|2=ja|3=雅楽寮}} - Music households: Gigaku (49), Wooden acrobats (8), Nara flutists (9) * {{ill|lt=Zōheishi|1=Zōheishi|2=ja|3=造兵司}} - Miscellaneous artisans: Nail craftsmen (18), Shield makers (36), Tent makers (16) * {{ill|lt=Kobukushi|1=Kobukushi|2=ja|3=鼓吹司}} - {{ill|lt=Kobukuto|1=Kobukuto|2=ja|3=鼓吹戸}} (Ōtsunebue) (218) * {{ill|lt=Shusen-shi|1=Shusen-shi|2=ja|3=主船司}} - Ship households (Ship maintenance households) (100) * {{ill|lt=Shuyō-shi|1=Shuyō-shi|2=ja|3=主鷹司}} - Falcon households (Falconers) (17) * {{ill|lt=Ōkura-shō|1=Ōkura-shō|2=ja|3=大蔵省 (律令制)}} - Koma households: Onukai Koma people (5), Takeshi Koma people (7), Village Koma people (30), Miyagori Koma people (14), Large Koma dyers (6), Cloth dyers (21), Asuka shoemakers (12), Wu bed makers (2), Cover seamers (11), Large kasa seamers (33), Tatami makers (72) * {{ill|lt=Urushibe-shi|1=Urushibe-shi|2=ja|3=漆部司}} - Lacquer workers: Lacquer workers (15), Mud wall decorators (10), Leather craftsmen (4) * {{ill|lt=Oribebe-shi|1=Oribebe-shi|2=ja|3=織部司}} - Dyeing households: Brocade weavers (110), Wu textile artisans (7), Kawachi silk weavers (350), Scarlet dyers (70), Indigo dyers (33) * {{ill|lt=Daizenshiki|1=Daizenshiki|2=ja|3=大膳職}} - Miscellaneous supply households: Fishermen (37), River people (87), Net makers (150), Soy sauce brewers (20) * {{ill|lt=Ōikeryō|1=Ōikeryō|2=ja|3=大炊寮}} - Cooking households (25) * {{ill|lt=Ten'yakuryō|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=典薬寮|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie}} - Medicine households (75), Milk households (50) * {{ill|lt=Sakabe-shi|1=Sakabe-shi|2=ja|3=造酒司}} - Sake households (185) * {{ill|lt=Enchishi|1=Enchishi|2=ja|3=園池司}} - Garden households (300) * {{ill|lt=Dokoshi|1=Dokoshi|2=ja|3=土工司}} - Mud households (51) * {{ill|lt=Shusui-shi|1=Shusui-shi|2=ja|3=主水司}} - Ice households (Water households) (114)
Their status was equal to that of ordinary public citizens (百姓, "hyakushō"), but they were considered slightly lower in rank. However, they had less strict government servitude, were registered in local census records as commoners, and were generally not treated differently. There were two types: hereditary '''Permanent Shinabe''' and temporary '''Borrowed Shinabe''' recruited from commoners. Some groups, such as Koma households and dyeing households, had annual tribute quotas, while others rotated members for service or supplied goods. In return, they received tax exemptions, including exemption from military service{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17}}.
In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period|2=ja|3=奈良時代}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō|2=ja|3=養老}} (721), the Shinabe of the Shuyō-shi were abolished, followed by a mass abolition in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Tempō Hōji|1=Tempō Hōji|2=ja|3=天平宝字}} (759), except for those with hereditary expertise, who were reclassified as commoners. Shinabe continued to be abolished, and by the {{ill|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period|2=ja|3=平安時代}}, only the Kobukushi of the {{ill|lt=Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|2=ja|3=兵庫寮}} remained. The decline was due to the incompatibility of Shinabe with the {{ill|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō system|2=ja|3=律令制}}, the decline of that system, and the rise of private industry, including former Shinabe artisans{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17}}.
Scholars sometimes refer to a combined system called '''Shinabe-Zakko System''' (品部・雑戸制) that includes {{ill|lt=Zakko|1=Zakko|2=ja|3=雑戸}}. However, Zakko had a distinct military role, was registered in a separate Zakko census, and had a stronger legal distinction from commoners{{Sfn|神野|p=15}}. [[Category:Shinabe clan forks]][[Category:New pages]]

== Overviewsuka shoemakers (12), Wu bed makers (2), Cover seamers (11), Large kasa seamers (33), Tatami makers (72) == * {{ill|lt=Urushibe-shi|1=Urushibe-shi|2=ja|3=漆部司}} - Lacquer workers: Lacquer workers (15), Mud wall decorators (10), Leather craftsmen (4) * {{ill|lt=Oribebe-shi|1=Oribebe-shi|2=ja|3=織部司}} - Dyeing households: Brocade weavers (110), Wu textile artisans (7), Kawachi silk weavers (350), Scarlet dyers (70), Indigo dyers (33) * {{ill|lt=Daizenshiki|1=Daizenshiki|2=ja|3=大膳職}} - Miscellaneous supply households: Fishermen (37), River people (87), Net makers (150), Soy sauce brewers (20) * {{ill|lt=Ōikeryō|1=Ōikeryō|2=ja|3=大炊寮}} - Cooking households (25) * {{ill|lt=Ten'yakuryō|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=典薬寮|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie}} - Medicine households (75), Milk households (50) * {{ill|lt=Sakabe-shi|1=Sakabe-shi|2=ja|3=造酒司}} - Sake households (185) * {{ill|lt=Enchishi|1=Enchishi|2=ja|3=園池司}} - Garden households (300) * {{ill|lt=Dokoshi|1=Dokoshi|2=ja|3=土工司}} - Mud households (51) * {{ill|lt=Shusui-shi|1=Shusui-shi|2=ja|3=主水司}} - Ice households (Water households) (114)
Their status was equal to that of ordinary public citizens (百姓, "hyakushō"), but they were considered slightly lower in rank. However, they had less strict government servitude, were registered in local census records as commoners, and were generally not treated differently. There were two types: hereditary '''Permanent Shinabe''' and temporary '''Borrowed Shinabe''' recruited from commoners. Some groups, such as Koma households and dyeing households, had annual tribute quotas, while others rotated members for service or supplied goods. In return, they received tax exemptions, including exemption from military service{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17}}.
In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period|2=ja|3=奈良時代}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō|2=ja|3=養老}} (721), the Shinabe of the Shuyō-shi were abolished, followed by a mass abolition in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Tempō Hōji|1=Tempō Hōji|2=ja|3=天平宝字}} (759), except for those with hereditary expertise, who were reclassified as commoners. Shinabe continued to be abolished, and by the {{ill|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period|2=ja|3=平安時代}}, only the Kobukushi of the {{ill|lt=Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|2=ja|3=兵庫寮}} remained. The decline was due to the incompatibility of Shinabe with the {{ill|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō system|2=ja|3=律令制}}, the decline of that system, and the rise of private industry, including former Shinabe artisans{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17}}.
Scholars sometimes refer to a combined system called '''Shinabe-Zakko System''' (品部・雑戸制) that includes {{ill|lt=Zakko|1=Zakko|2=ja|3=雑戸}}. However, Zakko had a distinct military role, was registered in a separate Zakko census, and had a stronger legal distinction from commoners{{Sfn|神野|p=15}}. [[Category:Shinabe clan forks]][[Category:New pages]]

== Overviewsuka shoemakers (12), Wu bed makers (2), Cover seamers (11), Large kasa seamers (33), Tatami makers (72) == * {{ill|lt=Urushibe-shi|1=Urushibe-shi|2=en}}[[ja:漆部司]] - Lacquer workers: Lacquer workers (15), Mud wall decorators (10), Leather craftsmen (4) * {{ill|lt=Oribebe-shi|1=Oribebe-shi|2=en}}[[ja:織部司]] - Dyeing households: Brocade weavers (110), Wu textile artisans (7), Kawachi silk weavers (350), Scarlet dyers (70), Indigo dyers (33) * {{ill|lt=Daizenshiki|1=Daizenshiki|2=en}}[[ja:大膳職]] - Miscellaneous supply households: Fishermen (37), River people (87), Net makers (150), Soy sauce brewers (20) * {{ill|lt=Ōikeryō|1=Ōikeryō|2=en}}[[ja:大炊寮]] - Cooking households (25) * {{ill|lt=Ten'yakuryō|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=en}}[[ja:典薬寮|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie]] - Medicine households (75), Milk households (50) * {{ill|lt=Sakabe-shi|1=Sakabe-shi|2=en}}[[ja:造酒司]] - Sake households (185) * {{ill|lt=Enchishi|1=Enchishi|2=en}}[[ja:園池司]] - Garden households (300) * {{ill|lt=Dokoshi|1=Dokoshi|2=en}}[[ja:土工司]] - Mud households (51) * {{ill|lt=Shusui-shi|1=Shusui-shi|2=en}}[[ja:主水司]] - Ice households (Water households) (114)
Their status was equal to that of ordinary public citizens (百姓, "hyakushō"), but they were considered slightly lower in rank. However, they had less strict government servitude, were registered in local census records as commoners, and were generally not treated differently. There were two types: hereditary '''Permanent Shinabe''' and temporary '''Borrowed Shinabe''' recruited from commoners. Some groups, such as Koma households and dyeing households, had annual tribute quotas, while others rotated members for service or supplied goods. In return, they received tax exemptions, including exemption from military service{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period|2=en}}[[ja:奈良時代]], in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō|2=en}}[[ja:養老]] (721), the Shinabe of the Shuyō-shi were abolished, followed by a mass abolition in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Tempō Hōji|1=Tempō Hōji|2=en}}[[ja:天平宝字]] (759), except for those with hereditary expertise, who were reclassified as commoners. Shinabe continued to be abolished, and by the {{ill|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period|2=en}}[[ja:平安時代]], only the Kobukushi of the {{ill|lt=Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|2=en}}[[ja:兵庫寮]] remained. The decline was due to the incompatibility of Shinabe with the {{ill|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō system|2=en}}[[ja:律令制]], the decline of that system, and the rise of private industry, including former Shinabe artisans{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
Scholars sometimes refer to a combined system called '''Shinabe-Zakko System''' (品部・雑戸制) that includes {{ill|lt=Zakko|1=Zakko|2=en}}[[ja:雑戸]]. However, Zakko had a distinct military role, was registered in a separate Zakko census, and had a stronger legal distinction from commoners{{Sfn|神野|p=15]]. Their status was equal to that of ordinary public citizens (百姓, "hyakushō"), but they were considered slightly lower in rank. However, they had less strict government servitude, were registered in local census records as commoners, and were generally not treated differently. There were two types: hereditary '''Permanent Shinabe''' and temporary '''Borrowed Shinabe''' recruited from commoners. Some groups, such as Koma households and dyeing households, had annual tribute quotas, while others rotated members for service or supplied goods. In return, they received tax exemptions, including exemption from military service{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period|2=en}}[[ja:奈良時代]], in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō|2=en}}[[ja:養老]] (721), the Shinabe of the Shuyō-shi were abolished, followed by a mass abolition in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Tempō Hōji|1=Tempō Hōji|2=en}}[[ja:天平宝字]] (759), except for those with hereditary expertise, who were reclassified as commoners. Shinabe continued to be abolished, and by the {{ill|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period|2=en}}[[ja:平安時代]], only the Kobukushi of the {{ill|lt=Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|2=en}}[[ja:兵庫寮]] remained. The decline was due to the incompatibility of Shinabe with the {{ill|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō system|2=en}}[[ja:律令制]], the decline of that system, and the rise of private industry, including former Shinabe artisans{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
Scholars sometimes refer to a combined system called '''Shinabe-Zakko System''' (品部・雑戸制) that includes {{ill|lt=Zakko|1=Zakko|2=en}}[[ja:雑戸]]. However, Zakko had a distinct military role, was registered in a separate Zakko census, and had a stronger legal distinction from commoners{{Sfn|神野|p=15]]. == Overviewsuka shoemakers (12), Wu bed makers (2), Cover seamers (11), Large kasa seamers (33), Tatami makers (72) == * {{ill|lt=Urushibe-shi|1=Urushibe-shi|2=en}}[[ja:漆部司]] - Lacquer workers: Lacquer workers (15), Mud wall decorators (10), Leather craftsmen (4) * {{ill|lt=Oribebe-shi|1=Oribebe-shi|2=en}}[[ja:織部司]] - Dyeing households: Brocade weavers (110), Wu textile artisans (7), Kawachi silk weavers (350), Scarlet dyers (70), Indigo dyers (33) * {{ill|lt=Daizenshiki|1=Daizenshiki|2=en}}[[ja:大膳職]] - Miscellaneous supply households: Fishermen (37), River people (87), Net makers (150), Soy sauce brewers (20) * {{ill|lt=Ōikeryō|1=Ōikeryō|2=en}}[[ja:大炊寮]] - Cooking households (25) * {{ill|lt=Ten'yakuryō|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=en}}[[ja:典薬寮|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie]] - Medicine households (75), Milk households (50) * {{ill|lt=Sakabe-shi|1=Sakabe-shi|2=en}}[[ja:造酒司]] - Sake households (185) * {{ill|lt=Enchishi|1=Enchishi|2=en}}[[ja:園池司]] - Garden households (300) * {{ill|lt=Dokoshi|1=Dokoshi|2=en}}[[ja:土工司]] - Mud households (51) * {{ill|lt=Shusui-shi|1=Shusui-shi|2=en}}[[ja:主水司]] - Ice households (Water households) (114)
Their status was equal to that of ordinary public citizens (百姓, "hyakushō"), but they were considered slightly lower in rank. However, they had less strict government servitude, were registered in local census records as commoners, and were generally not treated differently. There were two types: hereditary '''Permanent Shinabe''' and temporary '''Borrowed Shinabe''' recruited from commoners. Some groups, such as Koma households and dyeing households, had annual tribute quotas, while others rotated members for service or supplied goods. In return, they received tax exemptions, including exemption from military service{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period|2=en}}[[ja:奈良時代]], in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō|2=en}}[[ja:養老]] (721), the Shinabe of the Shuyō-shi were abolished, followed by a mass abolition in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Tempō Hōji|1=Tempō Hōji|2=en}}[[ja:天平宝字]] (759), except for those with hereditary expertise, who were reclassified as commoners. Shinabe continued to be abolished, and by the {{ill|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period|2=en}}[[ja:平安時代]], only the Kobukushi of the {{ill|lt=Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|2=en}}[[ja:兵庫寮]] remained. The decline was due to the incompatibility of Shinabe with the {{ill|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō system|2=en}}[[ja:律令制]], the decline of that system, and the rise of private industry, including former Shinabe artisans{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
Scholars sometimes refer to a combined system called '''Shinabe-Zakko System''' (品部・雑戸制) that includes {{ill|lt=Zakko|1=Zakko|2=en}}[[ja:雑戸]]. However, Zakko had a distinct military role, was registered in a separate Zakko census, and had a stronger legal distinction from commoners{{Sfn|神野|p=15]]. Their status was equal to that of ordinary public citizens (百姓, "hyakushō"), but they were considered slightly lower in rank. However, they had less strict government servitude, were registered in local census records as commoners, and were generally not treated differently. There were two types: hereditary '''Permanent Shinabe''' and temporary '''Borrowed Shinabe''' recruited from commoners. Some groups, such as Koma households and dyeing households, had annual tribute quotas, while others rotated members for service or supplied goods. In return, they received tax exemptions, including exemption from military service{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period|2=en}}[[ja:奈良時代]], in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō|2=en}}[[ja:養老]] (721), the Shinabe of the Shuyō-shi were abolished, followed by a mass abolition in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Tempō Hōji|1=Tempō Hōji|2=en}}[[ja:天平宝字]] (759), except for those with hereditary expertise, who were reclassified as commoners. The decline was due to the incompatibility of Shinabe with the {{ill|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō system|2=en}}[[ja:律令制]], the decline of that system, and the rise of private industry, including former Shinabe artisans{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
Scholars sometimes refer to a combined system called '''Shinabe-Zakko System''' (品部・雑戸制) that includes {{ill|lt=Zakko|1=Zakko|2=en}}[[ja:雑戸]]. However, Zakko had a distinct military role, was registered in a separate Zakko census, and had a stronger legal distinction from commoners{{Sfn|神野|p=15]]. == Overviewsuka shoemakers (12), Wu bed makers (2), Cover seamers (11), Large kasa seamers (33), Tatami makers (72) == * {{ill|lt=Urushibe-shi|1=Urushibe-shi|2=en}}[[ja:漆部司]] - Lacquer workers: Lacquer workers (15), Mud wall decorators (10), Leather craftsmen (4) * {{ill|lt=Oribebe-shi|1=Oribebe-shi|2=en}}[[ja:織部司]] - Dyeing households: Brocade weavers (110), Wu textile artisans (7), Kawachi silk weavers (350), Scarlet dyers (70), Indigo dyers (33) * {{ill|lt=Daizenshiki|1=Daizenshiki|2=en}}[[ja:大膳職]] - Miscellaneous supply households: Fishermen (37), River people (87), Net makers (150), Soy sauce brewers (20) * {{ill|lt=Ōikeryō|1=Ōikeryō|2=en}}[[ja:大炊寮]] - Cooking households (25) * {{ill|lt=Ten'yakuryō|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=en}}[[ja:典薬寮|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie]] - Medicine households (75), Milk households (50) * {{ill|lt=Sakabe-shi|1=Sakabe-shi|2=en}}[[ja:造酒司]] - Sake households (185) * {{ill|lt=Enchishi|1=Enchishi|2=en}}[[ja:園池司]] - Garden households (300) * {{ill|lt=Dokoshi|1=Dokoshi|2=en}}[[ja:土工司]] - Mud households (51) * {{ill|lt=Shusui-shi|1=Shusui-shi|2=en}}[[ja:主水司]] - Ice households (Water households) (114)
Their status was equal to that of ordinary public citizens (百姓, "hyakushō"), but they were considered slightly lower in rank. However, they had less strict government servitude, were registered in local census records as commoners, and were generally not treated differently. There were two types: hereditary '''Permanent Shinabe''' and temporary '''Borrowed Shinabe''' recruited from commoners. Some groups, such as Koma households and dyeing households, had annual tribute quotas, while others rotated members for service or supplied goods. In return, they received tax exemptions, including exemption from military service{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period|2=en}}[[ja:奈良時代]], in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō|2=en}}[[ja:養老]] (721), the Shinabe of the Shuyō-shi were abolished, followed by a mass abolition in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Tempō Hōji|1=Tempō Hōji|2=en}}[[ja:天平宝字]] (759), except for those with hereditary expertise, who were reclassified as commoners. Shinabe continued to be abolished, and by the {{ill|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period|2=en}}[[ja:平安時代]], only the Kobukushi of the {{ill|lt=Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|2=en}}[[ja:兵庫寮]] remained. The decline was due to the incompatibility of Shinabe with the {{ill|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō system|2=en}}[[ja:律令制]], the decline of that system, and the rise of private industry, including former Shinabe artisans{{Sfn|神野|pp=15-17]].
Scholars sometimes refer to a combined system called '''Shinabe-Zakko System''' (品部・雑戸制) that includes {{ill|lt=Zakko|1=Zakko|2=en}}[[ja:雑戸]]. However, Zakko had a distinct military role, was registered in a separate Zakko census, and had a stronger legal distinction from commoners{{Sfn|神野|p=15]]. Their status was equal to that of ordinary public citizens (百姓, "hyakushō"), but they were considered slightly lower in rank. There were two types: hereditary '''Permanent Shinabe''' and temporary '''Borrowed Shinabe''' recruited from commoners. category]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:Urushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== 概要 == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:大蔵省]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:Urushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== 概要 == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:大蔵省]]

[[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:大蔵省]]

category]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:大蔵省]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]

== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:大蔵省]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。

category]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:大蔵省]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]

'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]

'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。

『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]]
[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[Izumo Province ]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== See Also == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]]
[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[Izumo Province ]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。

[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== Overview == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[Izumo Province ]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。

[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

== See Also == * {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像}}

[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿}} and {{ill|Takeuchi Rizō|ja|竹内理三}}, Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965.
== See Also == * {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像}}

According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.

The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿}} and {{ill|Takeuchi Rizō|ja|竹内理三}}, Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965.
== See Also == * {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji}}

[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}

According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
