[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Urushibe-shi]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] <!--Urushibe-shi-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Urushibe-shi}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urushibe-shi]][[sn:rushibe-shi]] '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿}} and {{ill|Takeuchi Rizō|ja|竹内理三}}, Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji}}

<!--Urushibe-shi--> <!--Urushibe-shi--> <!--Urushibe-shi--> <!--Urushibe-shi--> <!--Urushibe-shi--> <!--Urushibe-shi--> <!--Urushibe-shi--> <!--Urushibe-shi-->
<!--Urushibe-shi-->
'''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō|12=draft}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令|12=draft}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Chō|ja|調|12=draft}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|12=draft}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=draft}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)|12=draft}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Ministry of Central Affairs|ja|中務省|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō|12=draft}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=draft}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeuchi Rizō|ja|竹内理三|12=draft}}, Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二|12=draft}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|12=draft}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|12=draft}}

6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:漆部司 up to rev 80608973
revision.text:
'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== 概要 == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。
『[[類聚三代格]]』によると[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日）、[[中務省]][[内匠寮]]に併合された<ref>『類聚三代格』巻4「加減諸司官員幷廃置事（雑任）15」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年正月20日勅</ref> 。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p758、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966年 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p914、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 岩波書店、1990年 * 『日本の歴史3 奈良の都』、[[青木和夫]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年
== 関連項目 == *[[元正天皇]] *[[丈部路石勝]] *[[丈部路祖父麻呂]] *[[東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ぬりへし}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:大蔵省]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[漆部司]] to [[Urushibe-shi]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''漆部司'''（ぬりべし/ぬりべのつかさ）とは、日本古代の律令制において[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]に属する機関の一つである。「[[職員令]]」により、雑塗漆事を管掌した。
== 概要 == 器物・仏像・絵画などの塗漆のことをつかさどり、漆室には[[漆]]を保管した。長官を漆部正（ぬりべのかみ）といい、佑（すけ）・令史（さかん）などの[[四等官]]のほかに、被官として[[漆部]]20人を擁していた。漆部は、「伴部」と「品部」からなり、漆工芸の製作にも携わり、[[調]]役が免ぜられていたという。
『[[正倉院文書]]』によると[[出雲国]]や[[遠江]]などの都から離れた地方にも漆部が置かれたという記録が残されている。仏教の隆盛は漆の消費を促進し，賦役として[[桑]]300根に対し漆100根を栽培することが義務づけられている。

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55533433]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Treasury|qq=|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q910073}} in Japan's ancient [[:en:Ritsuryō|ritsuryō system]]. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiinryō|qq=|1=Shokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996852}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying [[:en:Key (music)|Chō]] taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin Documents|qq=|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]] and [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ([[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|qq=|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Nakatsukasa-shō]].<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|qq=|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Rizō]], Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Keiji|qq=|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Kazuo|qq=|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|qq=|1=Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177897}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|qq=|1=Ojimaro Hasetsukabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177902}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55533433]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Treasury|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q910073}} in Japan's ancient [[:en:Ritsuryō|ritsuryō system]]. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiinryō|1=Shokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996852}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying [[:en:Key (music)|Chō]] taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin Documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]] and [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ([[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Nakatsukasa-shō]].<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55533433]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Treasury|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q910073}} in Japan's ancient [[:en:Ritsuryō|ritsuryō system]]. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiinryō|1=Shokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996852}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying [[:en:Key (music)|Chō]] taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin Documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]] and [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ([[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Nakatsukasa-shō]].<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Rizō]], Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Keiji|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Kazuo|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|1=Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177897}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|1=Ojimaro Hasetsukabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177902}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55533433]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Treasury|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q910073}} in Japan's ancient [[:en:Ritsuryō|ritsuryō system]]. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiinryō|1=Shokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996852}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying [[:en:Key (music)|Chō]] taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin Documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]] and [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ([[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Nakatsukasa-shō]].<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Rizō]], Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Keiji|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Kazuo|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|1=Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177897}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|1=Ojimaro Hasetsukabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177902}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55533433]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Treasury|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q910073}} in Japan's ancient [[:en:Ritsuryō|ritsuryō system]]. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiinryō|1=Shokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996852}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying [[:en:Key (music)|Chō]] taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin Documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]] and [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ([[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Nakatsukasa-shō]].<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Rizō]], Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Keiji|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Kazuo|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|1=Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177897}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|1=Ojimaro Hasetsukabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177902}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|qq=}}

According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ([[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Nakatsukasa-shō]].<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55533433]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Treasury|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q910073}} in Japan's ancient [[:en:Ritsuryō|ritsuryō system]]. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiinryō|1=Shokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996852}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying [[:en:Key (music)|Chō]] taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin Documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]] and [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ([[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Nakatsukasa-shō]].<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>

According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ([[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Nakatsukasa-shō]].<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Rizō]], Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Keiji|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Kazuo|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|1=Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177897}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|1=Ojimaro Hasetsukabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177902}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|qq=}}

{{wikidata link|Q55533433}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Ministry of Finance of Japan]] [[Category:Officials of the Ritsuryō System]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:漆部司]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55533433]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Treasury|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q910073}} in Japan's ancient [[:en:Ritsuryō|ritsuryō system]]. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiinryō|1=Shokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996852}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying [[:en:Key (music)|Chō]] taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin Documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]] and [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ([[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Nakatsukasa-shō]].<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Rizō]], Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Keiji|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Kazuo|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|1=Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177897}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|1=Ojimaro Hasetsukabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177902}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|qq=}}

Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Key (music)|en|Key (music)|WD=Q534932|lt=Chō}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin Documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Naishōryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and {{ill|Rizō Takeuchi|en|Rizō Takeuchi|WD=Q4262779|lt=Takeuchi Rizō}}, Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Keiji|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Kazuo|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|1=Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177897}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|1=Ojimaro Hasetsukabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177902}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|qq=}}

'''Nuribeshi''' (漆部司, ぬりべし or ぬりべのつかさ) was an institution under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|ja|大蔵省|lt=Ōkurashō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Treasury|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q910073}} in Japan's ancient {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. According to the {{ill|Shikiinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiinryō|1=Shokuinryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996852}}, it oversaw general lacquer-related matters.
== Overview == The Nuribeshi managed lacquerwork on various items such as utensils, Buddhist statues, and paintings, and stored lacquer in a special facility called the ''Urushimuro'' (漆室). The head of this office was titled ''Nuribe no Kami'' (漆部正), supported by other officials such as ''Suke'' (佑) and ''Sakan'' (令史). Additionally, the office employed 20 specialized craftsmen called ''Nuribe'' (漆部), divided into ''Tomobe'' (伴部) and ''Shinabe'' (品部), who were directly involved in lacquer production and exempted from paying {{ill|Key (music)|en|Key (music)|WD=Q534932|lt=Chō|1=Key (music)}} taxes.
According to the ''{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書|lt=Shōsōin Monjo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin Documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}'', records exist indicating the presence of Nuribe units in remote provinces such as {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province|1=Izumo Province}} and {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. The flourishing of Buddhism increased lacquer consumption, prompting regulations requiring the cultivation of 100 lacquer trees for every 300 mulberry trees as part of mandatory labor.
According to the ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}'', on January 20, 808 ({{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} year 3), Nuribeshi was merged into the {{ill|Naishōryō|ja|内匠寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishōryō|1=Q11393872|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393872}} of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Nakatsukasa-shō|1=Ministry of the Center}}.<ref>{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}, Vol. 4 "Addition and Reduction of Officials and Offices," January 20, Daidō year 3.</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kadokawa Nihonshi Jiten'' (角川第二版日本史辞典), p. 758, edited by {{ill|Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Mitsutoshi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and {{ill|Rizō Takeuchi|en|Rizō Takeuchi|WD=Q4262779|lt=Takeuchi Rizō|1=Rizō Takeuchi}}, Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten'' (岩波日本史辞典), p. 914, supervised by {{ill|Nagahara Keiji|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Keiji|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (続日本紀2), Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, Iwanami Shoten, 1990. * ''Nihon no Rekishi 3: Nara no Miyako'' (日本の歴史3 奈良の都), written by {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Kazuo|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}, Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. == See Also == * {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|ja|丈部路石勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Ishikatsu|1=Q55177897|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177897}} * {{ill|Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|ja|丈部路祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe no Michi Sofumaro|1=Ojimaro Hasetsukabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177902}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|1=Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Vairocana Buddha Statue|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Buddha of Tōdaiji|qq=}}
